content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
August 25,
1992
Robert E.
Roney
P.
0.
Box 800
Susanville,
CA
96130
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A-92-420
Dear Mr.
Roney:
You have requested advice on behalf of Wayne Tezak,
an
unsuccessful candidate for the Lassen County Board of Supervisors,
concerning the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political
Reform Act
(the “Act”).
1
?
QUESTION
May Mr. Tezak’s campaign disclosure reports be amended to
properly disclose his personal funds used in connection with his
candidacy as loans rather than monetary contributions?
CONCLUSION
Mr.
Tezak may amend the campaign disclosure reports to
disclose the contributions as loans.
FACTS
You are the campaign manager for Wayne Tezak,
an unsuccessful
candidate for the Lassen County Board of Supervisors.
Personal
funds which Mr.
Tezak loaned to his committee were inadvertently
reported as monetary contributions on the committee’s campaign
disclosure reports.
ANALYSIS
The Act requires candidates for state and local elective
offices to file periodic reports disclosing contributions received
and expenditures made for the purpose of being elected to
1/
Government Code Sections 81000—91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000,
et .
All references to regulations are to Title
2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800
•
P.O. Box 807
•
Sacramento, CA
958U
•
16) 322-5660


Robert E.
Roney
Our File No.
A—92-420
Page
2
office.
(Section 84100, et g.)
The term “contribution”
includes a loan.
(Section 82015.)
Unless there is an agreement or understanding at the time a
contribution is made that it will be repaid, the contribution may
not be reported as a loan.
However,
a candidate or committee
which erroneously discloses a loan as a monetary contribution may
amend its campaign statement to reflect the true nature of the
contribution.
(Paul Advice Letter,
No.
89—199.)
To accomplish
this,
Mr.
Tezak must file an amendment to each campaign statement
on which the loans were erroneously disclosed as monetary
contributions.
Amendment forms may be obtained from and should be
filed with the campaign filing officer for Lassen County.
If you have questions concerning this letter, please contact
me at
(916)
322_5662.2!
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
By:
Carla Wardlow
Chief, Technical Assistance and
Analysis Division
2/
Copies of Commission regulations and opinions are available
in many law libraries.
Alternatively,
copies of these materials
and Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission
at
a cost of l0 per page.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: May Mr. Tezak’s campaign disclosure reports be amended to
properly disclose his personal funds used in connection with his
candidacy as loans rather than monetary contributions?

CONCLUSION: Mr.
Tezak may amend the campaign disclosure reports to
disclose the contributions as loans.

Box 800 Susanville, CA 96130 Re: Your Request for Advice Our File No. A-92-420 Dear Mr. Roney: You have requested advice on behalf of Wayne Tezak, an unsuccessful candidate for the Lassen County Board of Supervisors, concerning the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”). 1 ? QUESTION May Mr. Tezak’s campaign disclosure reports be amended to properly disclose his personal funds used in connection with his candidacy as loans rather than monetary contributions? CONCLUSION Mr. Tezak may amend the campaign disclosure reports to disclose the contributions as loans. FACTS You are the campaign manager for Wayne Tezak, an unsuccessful candidate for the Lassen County Board of Supervisors. Personal funds which Mr. Tezak loaned to his committee were inadvertently reported as monetary contributions on the committee’s campaign disclosure reports. ANALYSIS The Act requires candidates for state and local elective offices to file periodic reports disclosing contributions received and expenditures made for the purpose of being elected to 1/ Government Code Sections 81000—91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et . All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 958U • 16) 322-5660 Robert E. Roney Our File No. A—92-420 Page 2 office. (Section 84100, et g.) The term “contribution” includes a loan. (Section 82015.) Unless there is an agreement or understanding at the time a contribution is made that it will be repaid, the contribution may not be reported as a loan. However, a candidate or committee which erroneously discloses a loan as a monetary contribution may amend its campaign statement to reflect the true nature of the contribution. (Paul Advice Letter, No. 89—199.) To accomplish this, Mr. Tezak must file an amendment to each campaign statement on which the loans were erroneously disclosed as monetary contributions. Amendment forms may be obtained from and should be filed with the campaign filing officer for Lassen County. If you have questions concerning this letter, please contact me at (916) 322_5662.2!
